architecture.summary: A two-stage framework that leverages Large Language Models to generate Game Description Language (GDL) from natural language. The first stage gradually constructs a minimal grammar based on GDL specifications. The second stage iteratively refines game descriptions through grammar-guided generation, using a specialized parser to identify valid subsequences and candidate symbols from LLM responses, ensuring grammatical correctness through iterative improvement cycles.

biblio.title: Grammar-Based Game Description Generation Using Large Language Models

baseline.name: baseline methods that directly use LLM outputs

claims.statement: Our iterative improvement approach significantly outperforms baseline methods that directly use LLM outputs
Generating GDL descriptions from natural language remains a challenging task
